data.title: Perion Network misses Q4 top-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Perion Network press release (NASDAQ:PERI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.33 in-line.
  • Revenue of $129.6M (-44.7% Y/Y) misses by $0.84M.

  • Financial Outlook for Full-Year 2025:

    2025 guidance reflects our commitment to continue building our Perion One platform while focusing on more profitable technologies and solutions that align with our strategy.

    The company is providing the following full-year 2025 guidance ranges based on current expectations:

    • Revenue of $400 to $420 million vs. $468.77M consensus.
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $40 to $42 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA to contribution ex-TAC of 22% at the midpoint

data.title: Stepan gives mixed Q4 results
data.description:
• Stepan press release (NYSE:SCL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $525.61M (-1.2% Y/Y) beats by $4.64M.
  • Global sales volume was down 1% year-over-year. Double digit growth in several Surfactant end markets was fully offset by demand weakness in Polymers.
  • Cash from Operations was $68.3 million during the quarter. Free cash flow(3) for the quarter was $32.1 million.
  • The Company delivered $13.0 million in pre-tax cost out savings in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Mortgage Applications slips after rising for two weeks
data.description:
• MBA Mortgage Applications
  • Composite Index: -6.6% vs. +2.3% a week ago.
  • Purchase Index: -5.9% vs. -2.3% the previous week.
  • Refinance Index: -7.3% vs. +9.6% in the prior week.
  • 30-year fixed-rate mortgages came in at 6.93%, compared to 6.95% last week

data.title: Manchester United Non-GAAP EPS of -3.65p, revenue of £198.7M; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Manchester United press release (NYSE:MANU): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -3.65p.
  • Revenue of £198.7M (-12.0% Y/Y).
  • Second quarter adjusted EBITDA was £70.5 million, down 22.9% from £91.4 million in 2Q24.

  • Overall cash and cash equivalents (including the effects of exchange rate movements) decreased by £54.0 million in the quarter to 31 December 2024, compared to a decrease of £18.0 million in the prior year quarter.

  • Net cash outflow from operating activities for the quarter was £63.2 million, compared to £46.6 million in the prior year quarter.

  • Net capital expenditure on property, plant and equipment for the quarter was £6.9 million, an increase of £4.1 million over the prior year quarter.

  • Net capital expenditure on intangible assets for the quarter was £49.9 million, an increase of £14.2 million over the prior year quarter.

  • For Fiscal 2025, the company reiterates its prior guidance of total revenues of £650 million to £670 million and now expects adjusted EBITDA guidance to be at the high end of its previously issued range of £145 million to £160 million.

data.title: Etsy GAAP EPS of $1.03 beats by $0.09, revenue of $852.2M misses by $9.17M
data.description:
• Etsy press release (NASDAQ:ETSY): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.03 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $852.2M (+1.2% Y/Y) misses by $9.17M.
  • Consolidated GMS was $3.7 billion, down 6.8% year-over-year and down 6.9% on a currency neutral basis.
  • Etsy marketplace GMS was $3.3 billion, down 8.6% year-over-year
  • Active buyers were 89.6 million, decreasing 2.6% year-over-year.
  • We reactivated a record 9.8 million lapsed buyers, up 1.3% from the prior year, and we acquired 6.9 million new buyers.
  • GMS per active buyer on a trailing twelve month basis was down 3.5% year-over-year to $121 in the fourth quarter.
  • Our number of habitual buyers was 6.4 million, down 9.5% year-over-year.

Q1 25 Guidance

GMS

We currently anticipate that consolidated first quarter 2025 GMS will decline at a rate similar to the year-over-year performance reported for the fourth quarter of 2024

Take Rate

~23%

Adjusted EBITDA Margin

~25-26%

data.title: ProPetro GAAP EPS of -$0.17 misses by $0.19, revenue of $320.55M beats by $2.93M
data.description:
• ProPetro press release (NYSE:PUMP): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.17 misses by $0.19.
  • Adjusted net loss was $596 thousand which excludes noncash impairment expenses.
  • Revenue of $320.55M (-7.8% Y/Y) beats by $2.93M.
  • Revenue was $321 million, compared to $361 million for the third quarter of 2024. The decrease in revenue is primarily attributable to our decreased hydraulic fracturing utilization caused by seasonality and holiday impacts.
  • The Company anticipates full-year 2025 capital expenditures to be between $300 million and $400 million. Of this, the completions business is expected to account for $150 million to $200 million, while an additional $150 million to $200 million will be allocated for growth capital expenditures in our PROPWR business. The Company expects to finance a significant portion of the PROPWR capital expenditures.

data.title: Yum! Brands takes KFC's headquarters out of its longtime home of Kentucky
data.description:
Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) announced that the KFC chicken chain would be moving its corporate headquarters from Kentucky to Plano, Texas. The restaurant chain operator said 100 KFC corporate employees will be relocated in the next six months.

The company said the decision to move KFC's headquarters is part of a broad plan to have only two brand headquarters in the U.S. The KFC and Pizza Hut chains will be headquartered in Plano, while Taco Bell and Habit Burger & Grill will remain based in Irvine.

Of course, the KFC chain was first known as Kentucky Fried Chicken. The concept founded by Colonel Harland Sanders in the 1930s. Sanders began by selling fried chicken at his roadside restaurant in Corbin, Kentucky, during the Great Depression. He perfected his signature blend of 11 herbs and spices, which remains a closely guarded secret to this day.

The first KFC franchise opened in Salt Lake City, Utah, in 1952. Today, KFC operates in over 150 countries, solidifying its position as a global fast-food giant. During Q4, the KFC Division generated revenue of $9.43 billion and operating profit of $377 million.

Shares of Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) are up 9.7% so far in 2025.

data.title: European markets mostly lower as traders read earnings, potential tariffs
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 6.40am ET.

London (UKX) -0.45% UK's inflation rises to 3% in January

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.69%

France (CAC:IND) -0.74%

Italy posted a current account surplus of €4,228M in December.

The current account surplus in the Euro Area widened to €50.5B in December.

Slovakia's current account deficit widened sharply to €1,245.1M in December.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.50% lower on Wednesday as markets assessed U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff talks, a host of earnings releases, and a hotter-than-expected U.K. inflation print.

Sectors and bourses were trading in mixed territory, after the benchmark's record close in the previous session.

On the corporate front, BAE Systems' (OTCPK:BAESF) shares slipped despite the British defense giant reporting a record order book, while Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) was trading lower after posting a year-on-year decline in adjusted earnings in its preliminary annual results.

Europe’s largest lender HSBC (HSBC) meanwhile reported an annual pre-tax profit of $32.31 billion on Wednesday and initiated a share buy-back of up to $2 billion.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points 4.57%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points at 2.55%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 6 basis points 4.62%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Gildan Activewear beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Gildan Activewear press release (NYSE:GIL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $822M (+5.0% Y/Y) beats by $16.25M.
  • Cash flow from operations of $211 million in Q4 and $501 million for the full year; free cash flow of $208 million in Q4 and $389 million for the full year.
  • Capital returned to shareholders of $246 million in Q4 and $889 million for the full year, through dividends and share repurchases.
  • Company announces 10% dividend increase for 2025.
  • Company initiates 2025 annual guidance.

  • For 2025, we expect the following:

    • Revenue growth for the full year to be up mid-single digits vs. estimated growth of 4.55% Y/Y;
    • Full year adjusted operating margin1 to be up approximately 50 basis points;
    • Capex to come in at approximately 5% of sales;
    • Adjusted diluted EPS1 in the range of $3.38 to $3.58, up between ~13% and ~19% year over year vs. $3.48 consensus;
    • Free cash flow to be above $450 million.
    • For the first quarter of 2025, net sales are expected to be up low single digits year over year. Excluding the impact of the Under Armour sock license agreement, Q1 net sales are expected to be up mid-single digits. Adjusted operating margin1 is expected to be in line with our full year guidance of 'up approximately 50 basis points'. Of note, the enactment of GMT in Canada and Barbados only took place during the second quarter of 2024. As such, on a comparable basis, the Company's adjusted effective income tax rate1 in the first quarter of 2025 is expected to be significantly higher than the 3.6% recorded in the first quarter of 2024.
